skill.name: looker-studio-bigquery
skill.description: Design and configure Looker Studio dashboards with BigQuery data sources. Use when creating analytics dashboards, connecting BigQuery to visualization tools, or optimizing data pipeline performance.
skill.summary: Create analytics dashboards by connecting Google BigQuery to Looker Studio with optimized queries, scheduled data refreshes, and interactive visualizations.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation-only content with no executable code. Static analysis flagged 89 patterns (external_commands, network URLs, filesystem references) but all are false positives - they are markdown code blocks showing SQL/bash examples for educational purposes, reference hyperlinks to Google documentation, and cross-references to other skill files. No actual command execution, network calls, or file operations occur.

content.user_title: Build Looker Studio dashboards with BigQuery data
content.value_statement: Transform raw BigQuery data into actionable business insights through interactive dashboards. This skill guides you through connecting data sources, optimizing queries, and designing effective visualizations that drive decisions.
actual_capabilities: Create and configure BigQuery data sources in Looker Studio
Write optimized SQL queries with proper partition key usage
Design dashboards using F-pattern layout principles
Implement interactive filters including date range and dropdown controls
Set up scheduled queries to pre-compute aggregated data
Configure row-level security and access permissions
limitations: Requires existing Google Cloud Platform account and BigQuery setup
Does not execute commands directly - provides configuration guidance only
Dashboard creation must be done manually in Looker Studio interface
Does not handle real-time streaming data sources

use_cases.title: E-commerce Analytics Dashboard
use_cases.description: Build a comprehensive sales dashboard showing revenue trends, conversion rates, and product performance with daily automated refresh.

use_cases.title: Marketing Campaign Performance
use_cases.description: Create multi-channel attribution reports comparing campaign ROI across Google Ads, social media, and email marketing channels.

use_cases.title: Executive KPI Scorecard
use_cases.description: Design a high-level executive dashboard with key business metrics, month-over-month comparisons, and geographic revenue distribution.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Dashboard Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Looker Studio dashboard connected to my BigQuery table. I need to show daily user counts and revenue for the past 30 days with a date filter.

prompt_templates.title: SQL Query Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my BigQuery SQL for Looker Studio and optimize it for performance. The query joins three tables and filters by date range but loads slowly.

prompt_templates.title: Scheduled Query Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to set up a daily scheduled query that pre-aggregates my event data into a summary table for faster dashboard loading. Guide me through the setup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user implementing data refresh automation

prompt_templates.title: Row-Level Security Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure row-level security in my Looker Studio dashboard so regional managers can only see data for their assigned countries. Show me the SQL policy and Looker Studio settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user implementing data access controls

output_examples.input: Create a SQL query for a daily revenue summary table
What filters should I add to my sales dashboard?
output: - Date range selector (last 7/30/90 days, custom range)

best_practices: Always use partition keys in WHERE clauses to reduce BigQuery scanning costs and improve query performance
Limit dashboards to 20-25 charts maximum and distribute complex analytics across multiple pages for faster loading
Implement scheduled queries that run nightly to pre-compute aggregations instead of using live queries on large tables
anti_patterns: Using SELECT * instead of specifying only required columns increases data scanned and slows dashboard loading
Placing more than 25 charts on a single dashboard causes performance degradation and poor user experience
Relying on live queries for large tables without caching strategy results in high costs and slow refresh times

faq.question: What permissions do I need in BigQuery to connect Looker Studio?
faq.answer: You need BigQuery Data Viewer role for read access, BigQuery User role for query execution, and BigQuery Job User role for running jobs. Share data sources with specific users or groups for controlled access.

faq.question: How can I reduce BigQuery costs when using Looker Studio?
faq.answer: Use scheduled queries to pre-aggregate data, select only necessary columns, leverage partition keys in filters, and utilize Looker Studio's 3-hour cache. Avoid SELECT * and live queries on large tables.

faq.question: What is the recommended refresh frequency for dashboards?
faq.answer: For most business dashboards, daily scheduled queries running overnight are sufficient. Real-time dashboards can use live connections but expect higher costs. Configure based on business needs and data freshness requirements.

faq.question: How do I implement row-level security in Looker Studio?
faq.answer: Use BigQuery row access policies to restrict data at the source level, or configure filtered data sources in Looker Studio. Map user emails to allowed values using SQL policies that grant access based on user identity.

faq.question: Why is my dashboard loading slowly?
faq.answer: Common causes include: no partition key usage, too many charts (25+), live queries on large tables, complex joins without pre-aggregation, and insufficient caching. Implement scheduled queries and verify partition usage.

faq.question: Can I share dashboards with external users?
faq.answer: Yes, but external sharing requires careful permission management. Share with specific email addresses rather than using public links. Consider data sensitivity and configure appropriate BigQuery and Looker Studio access controls.
